collection: CUL, T-S ; CUL, T-S ; CUL, T-S
description: Document concerning a settlement of accounts in which Hillel b. ʿEli the cantor was both party and scribe. Dating: 1066–1108 CE, based on the years when Hillel b. ʿEli was active. In which Musallam a.k.a. Meshullam [b. Shelomo] Ibn Sunaynāt (appears also in T-S 12.779 (PGPID 20076)) seeks to collect from Hillel b. ʿEli the debts owed by Hillel's son Abū l-Ḥasan ʿEli b. Hillel, who is a known troublemaker from other documents. There is mention of ʿAlam al-Dawla Ibn Quraysh (or Qadish, see ENA 4020.30 (PGPID 2407)). It seems the son has "notes" (riqāʿ) totaling 200 dirhams (so perhaps promissory notes?) with this amīr. (Information in part from Goitein's index cards.) Joins: Alan Elbaum
description_he: מסמך משפטי ביהודית-ערבית מהמאה ה-11. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
